
### allocations and lookup table 



::: {.r-stack .my-full}
![](/bounds/texfig/lkpTble.figure-1.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=1}

![](/bounds/texfig/lkpTble.figure-2.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2}

![](/bounds/texfig/lkpTble.figure-3.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=3}

![](/bounds/texfig/lkpTble.figure-4.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=4}

![](/bounds/texfig/lkpTble.figure-5.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=5}


:::


### managing the table


::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column}

* not just done <code>malloc()/new</code>
* also for stack allocations:

```c
void vulnerable() {
    char buffer[100];
    gets(vulnerable);
}
```

::::

:::: {.column}

```gnuassembler
vulnerable:
  // make %rsp a multiple
  // of 128 (2^7) 
  andq $0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFF80, %rsp
  // allocate 128 bytes
  subq $0x80, %rsp
  // rax <- rsp / 16
  movq $rsp, %rax
  shrq $4, %rax
  movb $7, TABLE(%rax)
  movb $7, TABLE+1(%rax)
  ...
  movq %rsp, %rdi
  call gets
  ret
```

::::

:::::

### sparse lookup table 

![](/bounds/texfig/sparseLookup.figure.svg)
